summary: Matched and linked the Mr. Game & Watch Rescue item source by moving src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchrescue.c from NonMatching to Matching, implementing it_802C8038, adding its ItemStateTable, correcting the spawn function return type to Item_GObj*, and replacing prior bombhei item-var union accesses with a dedicated gamewatchrescue item-var struct.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgamewatchrescue.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Implemented it_802C8038 as an Item_GObj* spawn helper for It_Kind_GameWatch_Rescue, initializing SpawnItem fields, calling Item_80268B18, zeroing item command vars, storing the parent in the rescue item vars, and invoking it_8027CE64 and it_802C8208.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F79C0 with two states using itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim, itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Added #include <melee/it/it_2725.h> to support the newly linked item code.
Added itGamewatchrescue_ItemVars in itCharItems.h and inserted it into the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h.
Replaced accesses through xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD8 with xDD4_itemVar.gamewatchrescue.xDD8 throughout the rescue cleanup and animation logic.
Updated itgamewatchrescue.h so it_802C8038 returns Item_GObj* rather than HSD_GObj*.
smart_moves: Introduced a dedicated itGamewatchrescue_ItemVars struct instead of continuing to alias the rescue item's xDD4/xDD8 fields through bombhei, improving type accuracy while preserving the same union offset usage needed for matching.
Corrected the public prototype of it_802C8038 to Item_GObj*, matching the implementation and the item spawn API pattern.

Kept the Game & Watch Rescue owner/parent cleanup behavior explicit: when the stored rescue parent matches item->owner, the code calls ftGw_SpecialHi_ItemRescueSetNULL before nulling item vars and removing the item.

Implemented the spawn path with fully initialized SpawnItem fields, including zeroed position z, velocity components, damage, parent object fields, and spawn flags, which is important for stable item matching and behavior.
decomp_lessons: When an item currently matches by borrowing another union member such as bombhei, add the correct item-specific ItemVars struct and union member once field meaning is known; this improves correctness without necessarily changing layout.
For item spawn functions, verify the return type against Item_80268B18 usage. Item object factories commonly return Item_GObj*, even if older headers used HSD_GObj*.
Matching an item file often requires coordinated changes across the C file, item-specific header, shared character-item variable declarations, the Item union in types.h, and configure.py.
Game & Watch article/item code may store a fighter parent pointer in item vars and must coordinate with ftGw_SpecialHi helper functions when the article is removed or invalidated.
State table reconstruction can be enough to link a file once animation, physics, and collision callbacks are already present.
naming_conventions: Dedicated item variable type added as itGamewatchrescue_ItemVars, matching the existing lowercase item-name convention used by union member gamewatchrescue.
Callback names preserved the existing placeholder style: itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Anim, itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itGamewatchrescue_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Unidentified item-var fields retained offset-style names xDD4 and xDD8 rather than inventing semantic names.
The state table symbol remained address-derived as it_803F79C0, consistent with unresolved global naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Switched configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only after implementing the missing function and state table.
Used a temporary void** for xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes and passed *tmp to it_8027CE64, suggesting the expression/type shape was selected to satisfy matching constraints.
Preserved explicit NULL checks and repeated GET_ITEM calls in cleanup paths rather than simplifying, likely to retain compiler output shape.
Used chained zero assignments for item command vars and velocity components where the diff shows exact source structure mattered.
Removed unnecessary HSD_GObj* casts after typing gamewatchrescue.xDD8 as Item_GObj*/HSD_GObj-compatible storage, improving source clarity while maintaining behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice. The context reports one issue comment but no body excerpt or review-comment content, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files still aliasing xDD4_itemVar through unrelated union members such as bombhei.
Search for Game & Watch item spawn helpers with incorrect HSD_GObj* return types that should be Item_GObj*.
Compare other itgamewatch item files for state table patterns similar to it_803F79C0.
Audit ftGw_SpecialHi item cleanup paths for consistent parent pointer nulling and ItemRescueSetNULL calls.
